one_time_description: Litigation financing for Husky Oil Spill claim and three specific claims against Government of Canada for historic breaches; loans drawn totalling $2,447,184 to fund litigation costs, expected to be repaid from settlement proceeds
economic_notes: First Nation is member of BATC Investments Limited Partnership (20% interest), FN Cannabis Limited Partnership (33% interest), BATC Land Holdings Limited Partnership (16.67% interest). Has Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. consolidated. COVID-19 impacted operations with increased pandemic expenditures and decreased BATC CDC and First Nations Trust funding. Class action drinking water advisory settlement eligible.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Province is Saskatchewan (SK). Consolidated entities include Sweetgrass First Nation CMHC Housing, Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership, and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. Sweetgrass Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and Sweetgrass Legacy Trust excluded from consolidation as Chief and Council do not control them. Significant litigation financing: four RBC term loans totalling ~$2.45M for Husky Oil Spill and three specific claims against Canada, secured by insurance policies, contingent assets not recognized. Class action drinking water advisory settlement eligible but amount not determinable. Health segment expenses show $0 actual (vs $39,901 prior year and $105,820 budget). No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC ($10,558,315) minus CMHC ($350,312) minus Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre ($178,743) = $2,385,447. Total government transfers includes ISC and CMHC only as Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre is a separate entity.
